title: Latest F1 News
description: Don't miss a Formula 1 moment – with the latest news, videos, standings and results. Go behind the scenes and get analysis straight from the paddock.

items.title: STRATEGY GUIDE: What are the options for the Barcelona-Catalunya GP?
items.content: Matt Youson takes a look at the different pit stop and tyre options that are available to the teams on race day in Spain.

items.title: What To Watch For in the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix
items.content: Michael Lamonato picks out five key things to keep an eye on when the lights go out on race day in Spain.

items.title: Red Bull drivers surprised by 'better than expected' pace
items.content: Max Verstappen and Isack Hadjar were left surprised by Red Bull's pace in Qualifying for the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix, with the pair set to start from the third row of the grid.
items.contentSnippet: Max Verstappen and Isack Hadjar were left surprised by Red Bull's pace in Qualifying for the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix, with the pair set to start from the third row of the grid.

items.title: McLaren will ‘have to push like crazy’ in Barcelona – Norris
items.content: Lando Norris narrowly missed out on a spot in the top three in Qualifying for the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix.

items.title: Antonelli admits ‘overdriving the car’ in Barcelona
items.content: Kimi Antonelli has shared his reflections after Qualifying at the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix, with the Mercedes driver having to settle for P3.

items.title: Hamilton assesses Barcelona chances after Qualifying P2
items.content: Lewis Hamilton believes "we’re in a good position to be able to fight" for victory in the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix, with the Ferrari driver set to start on the front row.

items.title: ‘I feel like my old self again’ – Russell on Barcelona pole
items.content: George Russell made a return to the top spot with Mercedes after delivering a spectacular lap in Qualifying for the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix.

items.title: Leclerc 'very ashamed' after Barcelona Qualifying crash
items.content: Charles Leclerc admits "feeling very ashamed" after crashing in Qualifying for the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix, adding that there was "no excuses" for the incident.
